codeReview.summary: The implementation fully covers the feature description. All required directories (core/, db/, services/, schemas/) are created with appropriate modules. GET /api/system/capabilities returns all 10 required fields with correct types and runtime GPU detection via torch.cuda. GET /api/system/health is refactored to use shared services and typed Pydantic schemas. 24 tests pass (10 new). Quality checks (ruff format, ruff check, ty check) all pass. The implementation is clean, well-structured, and follows the TDD approach specified in the backend-worker skill.

issues.description: get_db_connection() duplicates get_connection() from db/__init__.py — both create SQLite connections with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled. The only difference is dependencies.py uses Settings while db/__init__.py takes a path directly. This is minor since dependencies.py functions (settings_dep, get_db_connection) are currently unused scaffolding, but the duplication could cause drift. Consider having dependencies delegate to db.get_connection().

] which triggers GPU detection (torch.cuda.is_available()) on every health check. While not a problem for M1, this will be unnecessary overhead once model_ready is properly implemented in M3. Should be refactored when model loading lifecycle is implemented.

DATA_DIR is still read from os.environ at module level in system.py, duplicating the same logic in core/config.py Settings. The health endpoint could instead use the settings dependency. This is consistent with the existing pattern from before this feature (main.py also does this), so not a regression, but worth noting for future consolidation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created a library doc at .factory/library/backend-skeleton.md documenting the directory structure, endpoints, key decisions, and validation assertions. This is good practice and covers the skeleton well.

The pattern of module-level DATA_DIR = Path(os.environ.get(...)) is duplicated across main.py, api/system.py, and core/config.py. AGENTS.md doesn't prescribe which pattern to use (module-level constant vs Settings injection). The config module exists but isn't consistently used as the single source of truth for configuration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: main.py:8, api/system.py:10, core/config.py:12-15 all read NANOSCRIBE_DATA_DIR from env independently

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used HOST_PORT=8976 docker compose up to start the container on an alternate port. This is not documented in services.yaml but appears to be a one-time workaround. No update needed unless this becomes a pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: HOST_PORT=8976 docker compose up -d

summary: PASS. The FastAPI backend skeleton is complete and well-implemented. All required directory structure (core/, db/, services/, schemas/) is in place. GET /api/system/capabilities returns all 10 required fields with runtime GPU detection. GET /api/system/health is refactored with typed schemas and proper degradation. 24 tests pass with good coverage of both endpoints. Quality checks clean. Minor non-blocking issues: duplicated DB connection helpers between core/dependencies.py and db/__init__.py, health endpoint triggers GPU detection on every call, and DATA_DIR env var reading is not centralized through the Settings config. The implementation correctly follows the backend-worker skill procedure (TDD, quality checks, manual verification).
